book_source: Universe v2 35-account narrative book (synthetic_book.py), advanced to the anchor's story day via book_simulator.simulate_book(). No daily operating-artifact snapshot of generated drafts exists yet in this repo for a given anchor day, so drafts are generated live via this demo path rather than read from a prior sweep artifact, per this dispatch's Decisions section.

output.customer_draft:
Subject: Checking your Launch Plan timeline before it slips

Hi Dennis,

A couple of onboarding milestones on your Launch Plan are now past their target date, and the account is showing a yellow health signal. Before that adoption gap starts to affect your launch timing, I'd like to get us aligned on where things stand and who owns the next step.

Proposed focus for a short working session:
- Confirm whether the current adoption gap is putting your launch timeline at risk.
- Review the overdue milestones and agree on the owner for each.
- Identify the next module and any missing user roles needed to complete the rollout.

If helpful, you can grab a time directly here: Schedule a working session (https://calendar.example/schedule/fleetops-csm-working-session).

Let me know what works and we'll get the right people in the room.

Best regards
output.reason: Pinehill Transport shows a success plan that is overdue (success_plan_overdue, target_date evidence df3f3e70-7ed8-5d10-9b05-395fd3d1a0f1) alongside a yellow health signal (health_yellow, health_score evidence ae0a5970-d69b-5c18-ad60-8015524e565c), with the CRM status observation (4e2db753-f222-5f7b-8356-0a2f59fae1e3) supporting the current state. Priority also reflects low seat penetration and a feature depth gap, together scoring 62. Given the overdue milestones and yellow health, the matching gap play is to verify whether the current adoption gap is putting launch timing at risk, so a working session with the account owner is the right next step.

golden_exemplars.content:
Subject: Recap — Integration & Data Setup sync, Sept 12

The Dispatch Bridge connector is still failing to acknowledge about 12% of outbound dispatch events; that's the one blocker holding this phase up.

What we covered:
- Reviewed the acknowledgement-rate numbers from the last 7 days (88% steady state, target is 99%+ before we call Integration & Data Setup done).
- Walked through the three retry-timeout settings on your side that are the likely cause.
- Agreed the fastest path is a joint session with your IT team rather than another round of one-off tickets.

Action items:
- Northgate: confirm IT availability for a 45-minute working session — owner Teresa Kwan (your side), needed by Sept 16.
- FleetOps: Grace Okafor to prepare a connector-log export isolating the failed acknowledgements — owner Grace Okafor, due Sept 15.
- FleetOps: send the updated retry-timeout recommendation in writing ahead of the working session — owner Marcus Webb, due Sept 15.

Once the working session happens we'll have a clear owner and date for closing this out. Let me know if Sept 16 doesn't work and we'll find another day this week.

Marcus Webb
Customer Success Manager, FleetOps Platform
golden_exemplars.title: Post-meeting recap — the FleetOps house style

terminology.activation: the customer reaches the first stable week of data-backed operations
terminology.asset: a vehicle or tracked field unit
terminology.capability: a product workflow the customer purchased and can adopt
terminology.dispatch_bridge: the professional-services integration workstream that connects a customer's own pre-existing dispatch system to FleetOps during onboarding -- not a purchased module, and never confused with the Dispatch Automation module
terminology.launch_plan: FleetOps' four-phase onboarding methodology: Kickoff, Integration & Data Setup, Activation, Steady-State Handoff
terminology.module: one of FleetOps' eight purchased capabilities: Live Map, Route Optimizer, Driver Scorecards, Maintenance Radar, Insights Hub, Compliance Center, Fuel Analytics, or Dispatch Automation
terminology.play: a recommended CSM motion, not a commitment or approval

value_props.name: Operational visibility
value_props.summary: Bring purchased assets online so the customer can see adoption gaps early.

value_props.name: Workflow adoption
value_props.summary: Move teams from purchased capability to repeatable day-to-day use.

value_props.name: Owner alignment
value_props.summary: Make the next operational owner and blocker explicit before the timeline slips.

voice_rules: Name the operational blocker before asking for time.
Use plain language; avoid hype, discounts, pressure, or escalation language.
Ask for a concrete working session with the right customer owner.

untrusted_text_fragments: Integration with legacy dispatch system failing

output.customer_draft:
Subject: Meridian Fleet Group — aligning owners on overdue Launch Plan milestones

Hi Alicia,

As we track your onboarding, we're seeing one or more Launch Plan milestones now past their due date, and I'd like to get ahead of any timeline slip before it compounds.

Rather than trade emails, I'd suggest a short working session to review the overdue Launch Plan milestones (Kickoff, Integration & Data Setup, Activation, or Steady-State Handoff) and agree on a clear owner and next step for each one.

If you're open to it, you can grab a time that works here: Schedule a working session — https://calendar.example/schedule/fleetops-csm-working-session

Let me know if there's a better contact on your side to include, and we'll make sure the right people are in the room.

Best regards,
Customer Success Manager, FleetOps Platform
output.reason: Meridian Fleet Group is flagged for proactive customer action based on a red health-score signal (source_id 47f78943-7a8a-5bf1-9eeb-df74372085c7, observed 2026-06-21) and an overdue milestone due_date (source_id 30f4240b-e6c6-5bd2-b7b4-42a9b8d68cc1, observed 2026-07-15). The priority score of 5 reflects the arr_tier factor. Given the overdue milestone, the recommended motion is to draft outreach proposing a working session to review the overdue Launch Plan milestones and assign owners.

output.customer_draft:
Subject: Checking whether the current adoption gap puts your launch timing at risk

Hi Gregory,

Our latest health signal for Harborview Fleet has moved to yellow, and we're seeing a gap in how deeply your entitled modules are being used. Before that turns into a Launch Plan timeline slip, I'd like to get ahead of it with you.

I'd suggest a short working session to do two things: verify whether the current adoption gap is trending toward a timeline risk, and pick one entitled module to unblock its next adoption step so we're moving forward on something concrete.

If that's useful, you can grab a time directly here: Schedule a working session (https://calendar.example/schedule/fleetops-csm-working-session). Let me know if a different time works better and we'll sort it out.

Best,
FleetOps Customer Success
output.reason: Priority factors flag a yellow health signal (health_yellow, health_score observed 2026-08-05 per source b8275b07-a825-5c1b-86d3-5b8063dcaf18) alongside a feature depth gap (feature_depth_gap). CRM status updates (625c9e2b-353d-5bc9-b866-91b2c536d104 on 2026-06-08, 2f75e535-4d63-5289-8084-aa72fcf3b39a on 2026-07-01) and a tracked due_date (4af5a5c0-f51a-5a83-9217-141f4333c6db observed 2026-07-15) support proposing a customer working session to verify whether the adoption gap risks a Launch Plan timeline slip. Deterministic score is 30; this reason does not change it.
